ORDER - STIPULATION AND ORDER FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-8-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JORDAN ONG'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AND ORDERS DEFENDANT TO SERVE PLAINTIFF WITH FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER AND IMPOSES SANCTION AGAINST DEFENDANT OF $500.00 PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEY WITHIN TEN DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COURT TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DEFENDANT TO SERVE PLAINTIFF WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THIS ORDER FULL AND COMPLETE ANSWERS WITHOUT OBJECTION TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SET ONE AND ASSESSES SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00 PAYABLE BY DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEY WITHIN TEN DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-08-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JORDAN ONG'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - EMPLOYMENT LAW, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - EMPLOYMENT LAW SET ONE. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AND ORDERS DEFENDANT TO SERVE WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FULL AND COMPLETE, ANSWERS WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - EMPLOYMENT LAW SET ONE AND IMPOSES SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00 PAYABLE BY DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNY WITHIN TEN DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COURT TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-8-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JORDAN ONG'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED. THE COURT GRANTS THIS MOTION AS FOLLOWS: (1) DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO SERVE PLAINTIFF WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSE TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMITTION. (2) IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO SERVE SUCH RESPONSES WITHIN THE FIFTEEN DAYS ALL REQUESTS OF ADMISSION SHALL BE DEEMED TO BE ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WILL BE PRECLUDED FOR INTRODUCTION AN EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE ADMISSION (3) THE COURT WILL ASSESS SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT OF $500.00 PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL WITH THE (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COURT TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been continued from February 1, 2023, for continued Prove-Up hearing. Dept 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: C. Balanga. Not Reported.
Ali I. Bushra Esq., Robert O'Dell, Esq., appeared with plaintiff Jordan Ong, an individual.
No appearance by defendants Beenest, Inc. a Delaware corporation or Jonathan Chou an individual.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on February 1, 2023, setting Trail date for February 17, 2023.
All DOE's have been dismissed October 7, 2020.
Brian A. Sullivan CPA is sworn and examined.
Ali I. Bushra Esq. submits the following exhibits on behalf of the plaintiff Jordan Ong, for the Court's review:

Exhibit 5: Appendix A - Bee Token and stock damages report................ID/EVID

Ali I. Bushra Esq. testifies he spent 120 hours on the matter and charges $350 per hour, for a total of $38,000 for attorney fees. Mr. Bushra has been practicing law since 2015.
Robert O'Dell Esq. testifies he spent 68 hours on the case and charges $700 per hour, for a total of $47,600 for attorney fees. He has been practicing law since 2011.
After hearing the evidence presented and good cause appearing, the matter has been submitted.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Continued To Feb-17-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been continued from October 5, 2020 for continued Prove-Up Hearing. Dept 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: C. Balanga. Not Reported.
Ali I. Bushra Esq., Robert O'Dell, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Jordan Ong, an individual.
No appearance by defendants Beenest, Inc. a Delaware corporation or Jonathan Chou.
The Court informs Counsel we are unable to proceed without giving all parties proper notice.
The Court orders plaintiffs' counsel to attempt to serve proper notice of hearing to defendants and submit a declaration or written record of all instances of given notice to defendants.
Default has not been entered.
DOE defendants have been dismissed.
Plaintiff to provide defendant(s) with notice of the continued hearing date.
This matter is continued by the Court for good cause finding to Friday, February 17, 2023, at 9:30am via Zoom for prove-up hearing.

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Continued To Nov-09-20 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Beenest Inc., a Delaware corporation, and Jonathan Chou on September 28, 2020 and continued to today, October 5, 2020.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C Sandoval. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Ali I. Bushra, Esq., Robert O'Dell, Esq. and tamara Freeze, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Jordan Ong, an individual, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Beenest, Inc. a Delaware corporation, or Jonathan Chou.
Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint for Breach of Contract was filed on July 17, 2019.
Defendants Beenest, Inc. and Jonathan Chou's answer to the first amended complaint was filed on Octoer 3, 2019.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on December 4, 2019, setting Trial Date for September 28, 2020, which was continued to today, October 5, 2020 by the Presiding Judge in Department 206.
DOE defendants have not been dismissed.

Plaintiff Jordan Ong submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Employment Agreement.......................................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Supplemental Bee Token Agreement...........................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 3: Handwritten Equity Agreement dated 2/27/18.................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 3b: General Contract Agreement dated 8/27/18..................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 4: Valuation Report...........................................................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court CONTINUES this matter to Monday, November 9, 2020 at 10:00 a.m. for further Short Cause Trial/Prove-Up hearing. Plaintiff shall provide the Court with further documentation in support of his request for entry of Court Judgment. The Court did make a finding DENYING plaintiff's request for punitive damages, but has yet to rule on plaintiff's request for attorney fees and costs. Counsel may appear via CourtCall at the next calling of the case.

Plaintiff to provide defendant(s) with notice of the continued hearing date.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Assigned To Trial Court

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ali Bushra
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDECONFERENCE):
ALI I. BUSHRA, Esq. of the Bushra Law Firm (Tel: 949-482-3006) for Plaintiff Jordan Ong;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JORDAN ONG'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. Both counsel and the judge pro tem appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AND ORDERS DEFENDANT TO SERVE PLAINTIFF WITH FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER AND IMPOSES SANCTION AGAINST DEFENDANT OF $500.00 PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEY WITHIN TEN DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.

Court to sign the proposed order submitted by email by plaintiff's counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDECONFERENCE):
ALI I. BUSHRA, Esq. of the Bushra Law Firm (Tel: 949-482-3006) for Plaintiff Jordan Ong;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JORDAN ONG'S Motion To Deem Matters Admitted, And Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. Both counsel and the judge pro tem appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED. THE COURT GRANTS THIS MOTION AS FOLLOWS:
(1) DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO SERVE PLAINTIFF WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSE TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMITTION.
(2) IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO SERVE SUCH RESPONSES WITHIN THE FIFTEEN DAYS ALL REQUESTS OF ADMISSION SHALL BE DEEMED TO BE ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WILL BE PRECLUDED FOR INTRODUCTION AN EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE ADMISSION
(3) THE COURT WILL ASSESS SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT OF $500.00 PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL WITH THE (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.

Court to sign the proposed order submitted by email by plaintiff's counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ali Bushra, Esq. 949-482-3006 for Plaintiff.
No Other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BEENEST, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION, JONATHAN CHOU'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF JORDAN ONG'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer is overruled. The demurrer to the second and third causes of action on grounds of uncertainty is groundless. (See Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135 [demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored, and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond].)

Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled that Defendants unfairly interfered with his right to receive the benefits of the various alleged agreements by terminating his employment and failing to perform their obligations under those agreements. (FAC 44.)

Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for fraud is overruled. Plaintiff has pled with sufficient particularity each element of a claim for fraud. "An action for promissory fraud may lie where a defendant fraudulently induces the plaintiff to enter into a contract." (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 638.)

An Order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ali Bushra, Esq. 949-482-3006 for Plaintiff.
No Other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BEENEST, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION, JONATHAN CHOU's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' motion to strike is denied.

Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's request for punitive damages is denied. The Court has overruled Defendants' demurrer to the fraud cause of action.

Defendants' motion to strike plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is denied. Code of Civil Procedure 1021.5 authorizes an award of attorney's fees to successful parties in actions that have "resulted in the enforcement of an important right affecting the public interest." It is essential to such an award that "a significant benefit, whether pecuniary or nonpecuniary, has been conferred on the general public or a large class of persons" and that "the necessity and financial burden of private enforcement . . . are such as to make the award appropriate." (Code Civ. Proc. 1021.5(a),(b).) It is doubtful whether this action for unpaid wages and breach of contract, which is brought on behalf of a single individual plaintiff and seeks to recover substantial monetary damages, will be able to satisfy either element. However, because a prayer for fees under 1021.5 need not be pled at all, and it is premature to determine whether the bases for an award can be met, the motion to strike is denied. (See Snatchko v. Westfield IIC (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 469, 497.)

Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's claims for Bee Tokens and stock is denied, as the motion is an improper attempt to adjudicate the merits of the case and is not brought on a proper ground. (See Code Civ. Proc. 436 ["irrelevant, false, or improper matter"].) Likewise, Defendants' motions to strike Exhibits C and D is denied, as Defendants fail to set forth any basis as to why the exhibits are "irrelevant." Defendants' motion to strike the allegations regarding Mr. Chou is denied, as those allegations are relevant to Plaintiff's fraud cause of action.

An Order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
